conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical conditions for PBO were realized in 88 wt% THF–water
conditions.detector: density detection system DDS 70 combined with SICON LCD 201 RI detector
conditions.evidence_text: Critical conditions for PBO were realized in 88 wt% THF–water and for PHO in 92 wt% THF–water. ... The columns and density cells in all systems were placed in thermostatted boxes, in which a temperature (25.0[°] C for LCCC ... was maintained
conditions.notes: Semipreparative LCCC used as first dimension. Text also mentions 88.23 wt% THF in Fig 3 caption.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical conditions for PEO exist at 75.7 wt% acetone–water
conditions.detector: refractive index detector RI 2414 or SEDEX 45 ELSD connected to DDS 70
conditions.evidence_text: Critical conditions for PEO exist at 75.7 wt% acetone–water. ... The columns and density cells in all systems were placed in thermostatted boxes, in which a temperature (25.0[°] C for LCCC ... was maintained
conditions.notes: Analytical LCCC used as second dimension to separate block copolymer from unreacted MeO-PEO initiator.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.evidence_text: On most common octadecyl reverse phase columns, it could also be realized with a slightly higher content of THF (87–92% for butene oxide, 90–95% for hexene oxide) [73]. Under the conditions mentioned above, the hydrophilic PEO as well as the block copolymer elute in SEC order. If there is any hydrophobic homopolymer formed during synthesis, it should elute at the void volume of column. In this study we used an octadecyl column with 10 mm inner diameter for the semipreparative separation of block copolymer from homopolymer. As shown in Fig. 3, the block copolymer (fraction 1) can be reasonably separated from the homopolymer (fraction 2).
conditions.notes: Semipreparative LCCC used as first dimension.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.evidence_text: In the case of amphiphilic polymers the interaction parameters cA and cB of the individual structural units (A and B) may have different values. If both cA and cB are negative, the molar mass distribution (MMD) can be determined by size exclusion chromatography (SEC), with retention decreasing with increasing molar mass, and only a minor influence of chemical composition. SEC yields the overall MMD, with dual detection [54–56] one may obtain information on the chemical composition along the MMD [56–58]. SEC is however not capable of discriminating a mixture of block copolymers and homopolymers. In the case of higher polymers, gradient LAC [59–66] or LC under limiting conditions [67–69] can be applied. In the range of lower molecular mass, however, LCCC is the best approach to separate homopolymers from the block copolymer. In AB diblock copolymers (e.g. A = EO and B = PO or another alkylene oxide) one may use the following strategies: At the CAP for the unit A, a separation according to the block B may be achieved by a SEC or a LAC mechanism, while at the CAP for the block B, the length of the block A may be obtained in the same way. The application of these methods to EO–PO block copolymers has been described recently [20,45,70–72].
conditions.notes: Analytical LCCC used as second dimension to separate block copolymer from unreacted MeO-PEO initiator.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
